name: wilds-suite

env_axis.name: shift-type-per-sub
env_axis.shift_mechanism: 10 sub-datasets each with its own natural shift mechanism

ood_protocol.name: wilds-per-sub
ood_protocol.ref: Koh et al. 2021 ICML; Sagawa et al. 2022 (v2)

schema.suite_note: suite — 10 sub-datasets. Camelyon17 broken out separately as a core entry within the suite.

facts: 10 sub-datasets curated for natural shift; v2.0 unlabeled-data extension; Koh et al. ICML 2021.
hypotheses: No single method wins across all 10 sub-datasets; method ranking is shift-type-dependent.
WILDS's curation introduces a selection-for-clear-shift bias.
intuitions: A WILDS v3 may have been announced.
The WILDS-vs-Wild-Time naming confusion likely appears in the 2023-25 literature (these are distinct projects).
Foundation-model cross-WILDS results have probably been published.
unknowns: WILDS deliberately picked clearly-shifted environments; there is no established framing for how curator choice propagates to out-of-benchmark generalizability.
Profile coverage is sparse — no profile region has ≥3 WILDS sub-datasets, so within-region statistics are weak.
epistemic_state.boundary: A suite requiring descent to sub-datasets for routing; suite-level claims need cross-sub-dataset agreement, which is empirically rare.

representational_commitments: 10 specific sub-datasets curated for clear shift (selection bias)
Each sub-dataset's env labels are curator-chosen
v2.0 added unlabeled data for UDA — changing the protocol mid-suite

MATERIALIZATION STATUS: 8 of 10 WILDS sub-datasets in GCS — civilcomments (260 MB), rxrx1 (7 GB sha=886594f2...), globalwheat (9.6 GB sha=8b76b4ef...), amazon-wilds (6.6 GB sha=2a13eae6...), iwildcam (12 GB sha=b0e5d056...), fmow (51 GB sha=8c802795...), py150 (928 MB), poverty (13 GB sha=6ee249b7e29a8a79fce804d0ea6716fbb5b514809209a2836e967a02adaa5376). camelyon17 has its own manifest (wilds-camelyon17, pending re-download via VM after stalled Mac attempt). OGB-MolPCBA materialized within ogb-suite.
